sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Units = Account Equity / (Maximum Historical Loss / Optimal f). Example: $2,250 per unit represents the dollar amount per unit derived from $900 max loss / 0.4 optimal f. Trade 1 unit for every $2,250 in account equity.

source_rule_text: Trade 1 unit for every $2,250 in account equity, derived from the ratio of maximum loss per trade to the optimal f value
